prose:
Method provenance:
Property: Frontier orbital energies & HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`), exact two-electron integrals (density fitting OFF)
Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point calculation, thus DFT//input geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/backend: PySCF (version not reported by engine)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity status ok, trustworthy true; no warnings reported by engine.
Live log & files: calculation streamed to a live .out file in the working directory (path available on request); result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpjgm35lc0/live.json.

Results (key values):
  HOMO energy = –8.603 7183 eV
  LUMO energy =  +0.336 95037 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.940 6687 eV (headline value)

This gap is a Koopmans-theorem orbital difference, not an excited-state energy.

All reported numbers are reproduced exactly as returned by chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill run with the options shown in the cli_invocation above.
